title: Dren
content:
[p]**Age:** Dren age slowly. Many live beyond 300 years, remaining physically capable long after other species would falter.[/p]
[p]**Size:** Dren **stand between 5’6” and 6’8”**, their **frames sturdy, weathered, and shaped by the elements**. Their skin **carries the touch of the Spiral**—some appear **soot-stained, others ember-flecked, and some seem to waver like mirages in the heat**. Your size is **Medium**.[/p]
[p]**Speed:** Your base walking speed is **30 feet**.[/p]
[p]**Vision:** Your embered eyes grant you **Darkvision up to 60 feet**.[/p]
[p]**Ashen Endurance:** You have resistance to fire and necrotic damage.[/p]
[p]**Cinder Veil:** Once per long rest, as a bonus action, you turn into smoke until the start of your next turn, becoming intangible and immune to non-magical attacks.[/p]
[p]**Smoke-Walker:** You are immune to inhaled toxins, poisonous gases, and smoke.[/p]
[p]**Keepers of the Ember:** You have advantage on death saving throws.[/p]
[p]**Languages:**[/p]
[p]**Intro**
The Dren are an ancient people whose memory stretches further back than most written histories. Long ago, they lived high within the Ashen Spiral, where heat, entropy, and slow unmaking shape all things. They were not cast out, nor driven down by catastrophe. They descended by choice, step by step, adapting as the world grew heavier and more fragile beneath them.[/p]
[p]Today, the Dren gather primarily in Dreadmere, the City of Ash and Smoke, where the Spiral’s influence still lingers. They are a people defined not by conquest or collapse, but by continuity. Where others fear decay, the Dren have learned to endure it together.[/p]
[p]**Origin**
The Dren originated in the upper reaches of the Mortal Plane, close enough to the Ashen Spiral that its heat reshaped both body and spirit. Over centuries, they migrated downward, settling along faulted lands, volcanic basins, and smoke-choked valleys where others could not survive.[/p]
[p]Some scholars claim their longevity is a lingering effect of the Spiral’s influence. The Dren themselves offer a simpler explanation. They endure because they choose to.[/p]
[p]**Appearance**
The Dren are unmistakably shaped by proximity to entropy and heat, their bodies bearing the calm authority of those who have endured conditions meant to erase life.[/p]
[p]They are powerfully built, with broad shoulders, deep chests, and dense musculature that suggests long adaptation to gravity, heat, and labor rather than brute aggression. Their frames are neither bulky nor ornamental. Every line of muscle appears purposeful, like stone shaped by repeated stress rather than sculpted intent.[/p]
[p]Their skin is ash-toned and mineral-hued, ranging from cooled slate blue to charcoal gray and pale volcanic stone. Across their bodies run distinctive ember-striations, broad, irregular bands of darker pigment that trace shoulders, ribs, and arms like old heat scars or sediment layers. These markings are not decorative. They deepen in contrast when a Dren is exerting themselves, under emotional strain, or exposed to intense heat, as if their body remembers fire.[/p]
[p]The surface texture of their skin is matte and resilient, slightly rough to the touch, resembling cooled basalt rather than flesh. It does not burn easily, blister quickly, or scar in the way other species do. Old wounds often fade into darker seams rather than raised scars.[/p]
[p]Dren faces are severe but not cruel, with strong brow ridges, flattened noses, and tightly set jaws. Their features appear weathered even in youth, giving them an ageless quality. Hair is sparse or absent on the scalp, and facial hair, when present, grows coarse and close to the skin.[/p]
[p]Their eyes glow faintly, pupil-less or nearly so, with hues of ember-orange, coal-red, or dull amber. The light is subtle, steady, and constant, like coals beneath ash rather than open flame. In darkness or smoke, their gaze remains visible long after others fade from sight.[/p]
[p]When a Dren stands still, there is a sense of weight and presence, as though the ground itself expects them to remain. When they move, it is deliberate and grounded, with no wasted motion. They look like people who know where it is safe to stand when the world burns.[/p]
[p]**Culture & Identity**
Dren culture centers on community and persistence. They believe no individual survives alone, and that isolation is the first step toward extinction.[/p]
[p]They are:
	•	Welcoming to strangers
	•	Slow to judge
	•	Deeply practical
	•	Quietly philosophical[/p]
[p]Fire is not worshipped, but respected. Ash is not seen as ruin, but as proof that something once burned long enough to matter.[/p]
[p]Storytelling among the Dren focuses on survival, migration, and loss, not heroism. Elders are valued not for authority, but for memory.[/p]
[p]**Societal Roles**
In Dreadmere, Dren commonly serve as:
	•	Fireworkers and forge-masters
	•	Structural laborers in extreme environments
	•	Guides through ashlands and unstable regions
	•	Keepers of communal hearths and shelters[/p]
[p]Beyond the Moratl Plane, Dren are sought as:
	•	Disaster specialists
	•	Industrial safety experts
	•	Advisors in volatile regions
	•	Guides for those drawn toward the Ashen Spiral but afraid to approach it alone[/p]
[p]Some Dren continue to wander, believing that to stop moving entirely is to invite stagnation.[/p]
[p]**Naming Conventions**
Dren names are old, heavy, and functional, often tied to endurance or place.[/p]
[p]Given Names
Examples: Khar, Ennet, Dovren, Mael, Sura, Thren[/p]
[p]Surnames or Epithets
Often earned rather than inherited.
Examples: Ashward, Smoke-Born, Cinderfall, Longwalk, Hearthkeeper[/p]
[p]**Life in Orimond**
In Orimond, Dren communities form around dangerous places others avoid: volcanic slopes, fault zones, industrial furnace districts. They are respected rather than feared, trusted to remain when others evacuate.[/p]
[p]Their homes are communal, reinforced, and built to last rather than impress. The hearth is the center of all things.[/p]
